title: aira caryophyllea subsp. armoricana
intro: Aira caryophyllea subsp. armoricana, also known as Armorican Hair Grass, is a subspecies of perennial grass native to Europe, particularly in France and the UK. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, derived from its essential oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of Aira caryophyllea subsp. armoricana include soothing digestive issues, calming respiratory problems, and aiding in wound healing. Herbal preparations of this plant are often made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally or applied topically to harness its medicinal properties.
